name: d.school Micro-Cycle
description: Stanford d.school micro-cycle: Understand/Observe (fan) -> Define POV (waist) -> Ideate (fan + prototype) -> Prototype & Test (waist).
when_to_use: Human-centered design when you want empathy-first divergence and rapid prototype testing.

steps.name: Understand & Observe
steps.intent: Build empathy: observe and surface user needs, contexts and frictions broadly.

steps.name: Define POV
steps.intent: Consolidate observations into a single Point-of-View statement (user + need + insight).

steps.name: Ideate
steps.intent: Generate many diverse solution concepts; build a prototype for the most promising.

steps.name: Prototype & Test
steps.intent: Personas use the prototype; gather feedback and converge to refined requirements.
